notes: Evaluated 3 files generated 2026-04-20 (anthropic.test.ts, add-type-annotations.test.ts, add-catch-handler.test.ts). All are real TypeScript with correct vitest imports — massive improvement from v2 aggregate 0.38. Compilability docked to 0.80 because relative import paths are guessed (e.g. './providers/anthropic.js', './base.js', '../normalize.js') — Gemma infers them from source content but cannot verify they resolve correctly from the test file's location. assertion_quality 0.82: assertions are specific and meaningful (toContain, toBe, toHaveBeenCalledWith, rejects.toThrow). pattern_consistency 0.93: all three follow the same beforeEach/vi.restoreAllMocks/describe/it structure consistently.
hypothesis: v5 adds an 'IMPORT STYLE REFERENCE' step before generation: read 1 existing test file from src/__tests__/ and include its import block in the Gemma preamble. This gives Gemma concrete examples of the project's actual import path conventions, reducing guessed paths.
